title: Poll: Majority of voters believe AI risks outweigh benefits
summary: A new poll reveals that 52% of voters think the risks of AI surpass its benefits, highlighting growing public concern. This shift in perception could influence upcoming policy debates.
body:
A recent NBC News poll has found that a slim majority of American voters, 52%, believe the risks associated with artificial intelligence outweigh its potential benefits. This marks a significant shift in public opinion, as previous polls had shown a more balanced view. The survey also indicated that concerns about job displacement and ethical issues are driving this sentiment.

This polling data comes at a critical time, as lawmakers and tech companies are engaged in heated discussions about AI regulation. The findings suggest that the public may be more receptive to stricter regulations than previously thought. Comparatively, only 38% of respondents felt that the benefits of AI outweighed the risks, while 10% were undecided.

The results have sparked reactions from both sides of the aisle. Advocates for AI regulation are using the poll to push for stronger oversight, while tech industry leaders are emphasizing the need for balanced policies that foster innovation. The next steps will likely involve more public debates and potential legislative actions as policymakers grapple with how to address these concerns.
